mapping.a_element: demographic noise from finite-population birth-death fluctuations
mapping.b_element: sentiment shocks perturbing the news index

mapping.a_element: timescale separation of fast game dynamics from slow demographic drift
mapping.b_element: slowly evolving latent sentiment component versus fast short-range corrective feedback
longer residence times in optimistic or pessimistic regimes

brief_justification: Paper A's persistent states are produced by frequency-dependent game selection (Hawk-Dove payoffs) plus intrinsic demographic noise in a finite population, whereas Paper B's are an empirically-measured weakening of mean-reversion / lengthening of autocorrelation in a sentiment index with no game, payoff, selection, or population; the only genuine overlap is the generic pattern of a noisy bistable/metastable system with a slow-fast timescale split, not a shared governing equation or process.
